cards.back: A collection of elements that follows the Last In First Out (LIFO) principle.

cards.front: List the basic operations supported by a Stack
What data structure is used to implement a Stack using arrays?
cards.back: A single array where the top element is the last one added and the bottom element is the first one added.

cards.front: What is the application of Stacks in the implementation of Backtracking algorithms?
cards.back: Solving problems like N-Queens, Sudoku, and Maze solvers where we need to try different possibilities

cards.front: What is the application of Stacks in the evaluation of postfix expressions?
cards.back: Converting infix expressions to Reverse Polish Notation (RPN) where operators follow their operands
